dublinCore.dc:title: 107 HR 5131 : Public Lands Military Readiness Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Received; read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources

form.official-title: To extend military land withdrawals in Alaska, New Mexico, and California for a period of 25 years and to make technical corrections to descriptions for certain military land withdrawals, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Public Lands Military Readiness Act of 2025.

section.header: Extension of certain military land withdrawals and correction of certain land descriptions

subsection.header: Extension of withdrawal and reservation for military use of certain lands

paragraph.header: Yukon Training Area, Donnelly Training Area East, and Donnelly Training Area West, Alaska, and McGregor Range, Fort Bliss, New Mexico
paragraph.text: Section 3015(a) of the Military Lands Withdrawal Act of 1999 (title XXX of Public Law 106–65; 113 Stat. 892) is amended by striking 25 years after November 6, 2001 and inserting on November 6, 2051.

Section 2910(a) of the Fort Irwin Military Land Withdrawal Act of 2001 (title XXIX of Public Law 107–107; 115 Stat. 1339) is amended by striking 25 years after the date of the enactment of this Act and inserting on December 31, 2051.

Section 3011(d)(2) of the Military Lands Withdrawal Act of 1999 (title XXX of Public Law 106–65; 113 Stat. 892) is amended by striking 608,385 acres of land and inserting approximately 605,401 acres of land.
Section 2902(c) of the Fort Irwin Military Land Withdrawal Act of 2001 (title XXIX of Public Law 107–107; 115 Stat. 1336) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking 110,000 acres and inserting 117,710 acres; and

by striking as Proposed Withdrawal Land on the map entitled National Training Center—Proposed Withdrawal of Public Lands for Training Purposes, dated September 21, 2000 and inserting on the map entitled Fort Irwin Withdrawal dated February 28, 2025.

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives December 9, 2025.
